title: Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Assuming gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)
# Nipple distance = 26 cm (52 stitches apart -> Peaks at X=20 and X=72)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:25)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # The base band (Rows 1 to 4)
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # Left Cup: Centers at stitch 20. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (39 - (Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup: Centers at stitch 72. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (53 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (91 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Band" = "#2C3E50", "Left Cup" = "#E74C3C", "Right Cup" = "#E74C3C")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + # Keeps our stitches square for the visualization
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

title: Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Assuming gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)
# Nipple distance = 26 cm (52 stitches apart -> Peaks at X=20 and X=72)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:25)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # The base band (Rows 1 to 4)

      # Left Cup: Centers at stitch 20. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (39 - (Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup: Centers at stitch 72. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (53 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (91 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"

  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map

  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + # Keeps our stitches square for the visualization
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

title: Underboob Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: 2 HDC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)

Base Band

Row 1: Foundation HDC 92 (or Ch 93, HDC in 2nd ch from hook and each ch across). (92 sts)
Rows 2 - 4: Ch 1, turn. HDC in each st across. (92 sts)



Left Cup
Worked over the first 39 stitches of the Base Band.

Row 5: With the right side facing you, skip the first stitch. Attach yarn to the 2nd stitch of the Base Band. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the remaining stitches unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6 (turn, Sl St in the first st, Ch 1, HDC across leaving the last st unworked). Each row will decrease by 2 stitches (1 at the beginning, 1 at the end).
Final Row (Row 23): 1 HDC remains. Fasten off.



Right Cup
Worked over stitches 53 - 91 of the Base Band (leaving 13 stitches unworked between the cups).

Row 5: Skip 13 sts after the base of the Left Cup (sts 40-52). Attach yarn to stitch 54. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the last st (st 92) unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6, decreasing by 2 sts per row (skipping the first and last stitch) until 1 stitch remains. Fasten off.

title: Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the pattern directory.

Underboob Top
Keyhole Underboob Top
Men’s Classic DC T-Shirt (Size M)

title: Crochet Vocabulary & Abbreviations
text:
Welcome to the glossary of terms used across our technical crochet patterns.

Stitches & Techniques

HDC (Half Double Crochet)
A medium-height stitch that is taller than a single crochet but shorter than a double crochet. It creates a solid, slightly textured fabric that is perfect for underboob tops and bralettes as it balances coverage and drape.


Sl St (Slip Stitch)
A very flat, low-profile stitch. In these patterns, it is primarily used to move the yarn across the work without adding height, which creates clean, stepped decreases along the armpit edges.


Ch (Chain)
The foundation of crochet. Chains are used to create the starting row, to add height at the beginning of a new row (a turning chain), or to bridge gaps in the fabric (like the center of a keyhole cutout).


St / Sts (Stitch / Stitches)
The individual loops or “V” shapes along the top edge of your fabric.



Pattern Anatomy

Inner Edge / Cleavage Edge
The side of the cup that faces the center of the chest. In our patterns, this edge often features increases to curve the fabric inwards and create coverage.


Outer Edge / Armpit Edge
The side of the cup that faces your arm. This edge typically features steady decreases to taper the fabric from the base band up to the strap.

title: Technical Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the Technical Crochet Pattern visualization repository.
Here you will find plain-text crochet measurements and stitch descriptions translated into structured R data matrices, visualized using ggplot2.

title: Men’s Classic DC T-Shirt (Size M)
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Measurements:
# Base Width: 100 stitches (~50 cm for size M)
# Height: 60 rows (~72 cm length)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:100, Row = 1:60)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    LeftEdge = case_when(
      Row <= 40 ~ 1,
      Row == 41 ~ 7,
      Row == 42 ~ 9,
      Row == 43 ~ 10,
      Row >= 44 ~ 11
    ),
    RightEdge = case_when(
      Row <= 40 ~ 100,
      Row == 41 ~ 94,
      Row == 42 ~ 92,
      Row == 43 ~ 91,
      Row >= 44 ~ 90
    ),
    InLeftShoulder = Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= case_when(
      Row <= 51 ~ RightEdge,
      Row == 52 ~ 38,
      Row == 53 ~ 37,
      Row == 54 ~ 36,
      Row == 55 ~ 35,
      Row >= 56 ~ 34
    ),
    InRightShoulder = Stitch <= RightEdge & Stitch >= case_when(
      Row <= 51 ~ 0,
      Row == 52 ~ 63,
      Row == 53 ~ 64,
      Row == 54 ~ 65,
      Row == 55 ~ 66,
      Row >= 56 ~ 67
    ),
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Bottom Hem",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 40 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Main Body",
      Row >= 41 & Row <= 51 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Upper Chest",
      Row >= 52 & Row <= 60 & InLeftShoulder ~ "Left Shoulder",
      Row >= 52 & Row <= 60 & InRightShoulder ~ "Right Shoulder",
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 51.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 2.5, label = "Bottom Hem\n100 sts x 4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 22.5, label = "Main Body\n100 sts x 36 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 46, label = "Upper Chest (Armhole decreases)\n80 sts wide x 11 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 22, y = 56, label = "Left Shoulder\n24 sts x 9 rows", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 78, y = 56, label = "Right Shoulder\n24 sts x 9 rows", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Bottom Hem" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Main Body" = "#1ABC9C", 
    "Upper Chest" = "#F39C12",
    "Left Shoulder" = "#8E44AD",
    "Right Shoulder" = "#8E44AD"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Stitch Density Map: Men's DC T-Shirt (Size M)",
    subtitle = "Front Panel (Standard DC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 100 sts)", 
    y = "Row Count (Length: 60 rows)"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

title: Men’s Classic DC T-Shirt (Size M)
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Measurements:
# Base Width: 100 stitches (~50 cm for size M)
# Height: 60 rows (~72 cm length)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:100, Row = 1:60)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    LeftEdge = case_when(
      Row <= 40 ~ 1,
      Row == 41 ~ 7,
      Row == 42 ~ 9,
      Row == 43 ~ 10,
      Row >= 44 ~ 11
    ),
    RightEdge = case_when(
      Row <= 40 ~ 100,
      Row == 41 ~ 94,
      Row == 42 ~ 92,
      Row == 43 ~ 91,
      Row >= 44 ~ 90
    ),
    InLeftShoulder = Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= case_when(
      Row <= 51 ~ RightEdge,
      Row == 52 ~ 38,
      Row == 53 ~ 37,
      Row == 54 ~ 36,
      Row == 55 ~ 35,
      Row >= 56 ~ 34
    ),
    InRightShoulder = Stitch <= RightEdge & Stitch >= case_when(
      Row <= 51 ~ 0,
      Row == 52 ~ 63,
      Row == 53 ~ 64,
      Row == 54 ~ 65,
      Row == 55 ~ 66,
      Row >= 56 ~ 67
    ),
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Bottom Hem",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 40 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Main Body",
      Row >= 41 & Row <= 51 & Stitch >= LeftEdge & Stitch <= RightEdge ~ "Upper Chest",
      Row >= 52 & Row <= 60 & InLeftShoulder ~ "Left Shoulder",
      Row >= 52 & Row <= 60 & InRightShoulder ~ "Right Shoulder",
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 40.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 51.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 2.5, label = "Bottom Hem\n100 sts x 4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 22.5, label = "Main Body\n100 sts x 36 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 50, y = 46, label = "Upper Chest (Armhole decreases)\n80 sts wide x 11 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 22, y = 56, label = "Left Shoulder\n24 sts x 9 rows", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 78, y = 56, label = "Right Shoulder\n24 sts x 9 rows", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Bottom Hem" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Main Body" = "#1ABC9C", 
    "Upper Chest" = "#F39C12",
    "Left Shoulder" = "#8E44AD",
    "Right Shoulder" = "#8E44AD"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Stitch Density Map: Men's DC T-Shirt (Size M)",
    subtitle = "Front Panel (Standard DC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 100 sts)", 
    y = "Row Count (Length: 60 rows)"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

title: Men’s Classic DC T-Shirt (Size M)
text:
Written Pattern (Front Panel)
Gauge: 2 DC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: DC (Double Crochet), Ch (Chain), DC2Tog (Double Crochet 2 Together)
Note: This is worked entirely in standard Double Crochet for a classic, breathable t-shirt texture.

1. Bottom Hem

Row 1: Foundation DC 100. (Or Ch 102, DC in 3rd ch from hook and each ch across). (100 sts)
Rows 2 - 4: Ch 2, turn, DC across. (100 sts)



2. Main Body

Rows 5 - 40: Ch 2, turn, DC across. You are creating a straight tube until you reach the armpits. (100 sts)



3. Upper Chest & Armhole Shaping

Row 41: Turn, slip stitch across the first 6 sts. Ch 2, DC in the next 88 sts. Leave the last 6 sts unworked. (88 sts)
Row 42: Ch 2, turn. DC decrease (DC2Tog) across first 2 sts. DC across until last 2 sts. DC2Tog. (86 sts)
Row 43: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog across first 2 sts. DC across until last 2 sts. DC2Tog. (84 sts)
Row 44: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog across first 2 sts. DC across until last 2 sts. DC2Tog. (82 sts)
Row 45: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog across first 2 sts. DC across until last 2 sts. DC2Tog. (80 sts)
Rows 46 - 51: Ch 2, turn, DC across. The armhole decrease is now complete. (80 sts)



4. Shoulders & Neckline
You will split the work to form the left and right shoulder straps.
Left Shoulder: - Row 52: With right side facing, Ch 2, DC across the first 28 sts. Leave the rest unworked. (28 sts) - Row 53: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. DC across remaining 26 sts. (27 sts) - Row 54: Ch 2, turn. DC across 25 sts, DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. (26 sts) - Row 55: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. DC across remaining 24 sts. (25 sts) - Row 56: Ch 2, turn. DC across 23 sts, DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. (24 sts) - Rows 57 - 60: Ch 2, turn, DC across. Fasten off after Row 60. (24 sts)
Right Shoulder: - Row 52: Skip the 24 center stitches of the neckline. Attach yarn to the 53rd stitch from the left armhole edge (or count 28 sts from the right armhole edge). Ch 2, DC in the 28 sts to the end. (28 sts) - Row 53: Ch 2, turn. DC across 26 sts, DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. (27 sts) - Row 54: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. DC across remaining 25 sts. (26 sts) - Row 55: Ch 2, turn. DC across 24 sts, DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. (25 sts) - Row 56: Ch 2, turn. DC2Tog at the inner neck edge. DC across remaining 23 sts. (24 sts) - Rows 57 - 60: Ch 2, turn, DC across. Fasten off after Row 60. (24 sts)
Back Panel: Work the back panel identically, but skip the neckline shaping. Simply work Rows 52-60 straight across all 80 stitches. Seam the panels at the shoulders and sides!

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Measurements:
# Base: 96 stitches
# Nipple centers: roughly 26cm apart. 
# Left center ~ X=24, Right center ~ X=72. Y-axis peak ~ Row 18.

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:96, Row = 1:30)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Define the physical shape of the top (reverting to the arched window look)
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 12 & Stitch <= (20 + 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 12 & Stitch >= (77 - 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Right)",
      Row >= 13 & Row <= 16 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-12)) & Stitch <= (96 - (Row-12)) ~ "Center Bridge",
      Row >= 17 & Stitch >= (5 + (Row-16)) & Stitch <= (46 - 1.5*(Row-16)) ~ "Upper Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 17 & Stitch >= (51 + 1.5*(Row-16)) & Stitch <= (92 - (Row-16)) ~ "Upper Cup (Right)",
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    ),
    
    # 2. Define the stitch type based on location
    Stitch_Type = case_when(
      Part == "Empty Space" ~ NA_character_,
      sqrt((Stitch - 24)^2 + (Row - 18)^2) <= 8 ~ "Dense HDC (Coverage)",
      sqrt((Stitch - 72)^2 + (Row - 18)^2) <= 8 ~ "Dense HDC (Coverage)",
      TRUE ~ "Standard DC (Breathable)"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part, alpha = Stitch_Type)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  # Adding structural boundary lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 12.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 16.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  
  # Adding labels for regions
  annotate("label", x = 48, y = 2.5, label = "Base Band\n96 sts x 4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 16, y = 8.5, label = "Left Lower Cup\n8 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 80, y = 8.5, label = "Right Lower Cup\n8 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("text", x = 48, y = 8.5, label = "Keyhole Arch", size = 3, fontface = "italic", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("label", x = 48, y = 14.5, label = "Center Bridge\n4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 25, y = 24, label = "Left Upper Cup\n14 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 71, y = 24, label = "Right Upper Cup\n14 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Base Band" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Lower Cup (Left)" = "#E74C3C", 
    "Lower Cup (Right)" = "#E74C3C",
    "Center Bridge" = "#F1C40F",
    "Upper Cup (Left)" = "#3498DB",
    "Upper Cup (Right)" = "#3498DB"
  )) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(
    "Standard DC (Breathable)" = 0.4, 
    "Dense HDC (Coverage)" = 1
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Stitch Density Map: Keyhole Top",
    subtitle = "Original Colors with Standard DC vs Dense HDC (Opacity)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 96 sts)", 
    y = "Row Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Measurements:
# Base: 96 stitches
# Nipple centers: roughly 26cm apart. 
# Left center ~ X=24, Right center ~ X=72. Y-axis peak ~ Row 18.

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:96, Row = 1:30)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Define the physical shape of the top (reverting to the arched window look)
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 12 & Stitch <= (20 + 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 12 & Stitch >= (77 - 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Right)",
      Row >= 13 & Row <= 16 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-12)) & Stitch <= (96 - (Row-12)) ~ "Center Bridge",
      Row >= 17 & Stitch >= (5 + (Row-16)) & Stitch <= (46 - 1.5*(Row-16)) ~ "Upper Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 17 & Stitch >= (51 + 1.5*(Row-16)) & Stitch <= (92 - (Row-16)) ~ "Upper Cup (Right)",
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    ),
    
    # 2. Define the stitch type based on location
    Stitch_Type = case_when(
      Part == "Empty Space" ~ NA_character_,
      sqrt((Stitch - 24)^2 + (Row - 18)^2) <= 8 ~ "Dense HDC (Coverage)",
      sqrt((Stitch - 72)^2 + (Row - 18)^2) <= 8 ~ "Dense HDC (Coverage)",
      TRUE ~ "Standard DC (Breathable)"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part, alpha = Stitch_Type)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  # Adding structural boundary lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 12.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 16.5, color = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  
  # Adding labels for regions
  annotate("label", x = 48, y = 2.5, label = "Base Band\n96 sts x 4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 16, y = 8.5, label = "Left Lower Cup\n8 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 80, y = 8.5, label = "Right Lower Cup\n8 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("text", x = 48, y = 8.5, label = "Keyhole Arch", size = 3, fontface = "italic", color = "grey40") +
  annotate("label", x = 48, y = 14.5, label = "Center Bridge\n4 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 25, y = 24, label = "Left Upper Cup\n14 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  annotate("label", x = 71, y = 24, label = "Right Upper Cup\n14 rows", size = 3, fontface = "bold", fill = "#FFFFFFCC", label.size = NA) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Base Band" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Lower Cup (Left)" = "#E74C3C", 
    "Lower Cup (Right)" = "#E74C3C",
    "Center Bridge" = "#F1C40F",
    "Upper Cup (Left)" = "#3498DB",
    "Upper Cup (Right)" = "#3498DB"
  )) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(
    "Standard DC (Breathable)" = 0.4, 
    "Dense HDC (Coverage)" = 1
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Stitch Density Map: Keyhole Top",
    subtitle = "Original Colors with Standard DC vs Dense HDC (Opacity)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 96 sts)", 
    y = "Row Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: Standard DC base with dense HDC coverage zones.
Stitches Used: DC (Double Crochet), HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)
Note: The pattern primarily uses Double Crochet (DC) for a breathable, looser mesh. However, around the center bust (spanning an 8-stitch radius around X=24 and X=72 on the map, roughly Rows 10-26), you will switch to Half Double Crochet (HDC) to create dense, solid coverage zones. Refer to the density map for the exact placement of HDC stitches row by row.

1. Base Band

Rows 1 - 4: Foundation DC 96. (Or Ch 98, DC in 3rd ch from hook and each ch across). For rows 2-4: Ch 2, turn, DC across. (96 sts)



2. The Cutout Region (Sides of the Window)
You will work the left and right sides separately to form the arching keyhole.
Left Side: - Row 5: With right side facing, Ch 2. DC/HDC (following the map for dense zones) in the first 20 sts. Leave the rest unworked. (20 sts) - Rows 6 - 12: Ch 2, turn. On the inner edge (the cleavage side), increase by 2 sts per row. DC/HDC in each remaining st. By Row 12, you will have grown to 34 sts. Fasten off after Row 12.
Right Side: - Row 5: Skip the next 56 sts of the Base Band (this wide gap forms the bottom of the arched keyhole window). Attach yarn to the 77th stitch. Ch 2, DC/HDC in the remaining 20 sts. (20 sts) - Rows 6 - 12: Ch 2, turn. Increase by 2 sts on the inner cleavage edge per row. DC/HDC in each remaining st. Do not fasten off; you will continue from here with 34 sts.


3. The Sternum Bridge (Closing the Window)

Row 13: (Continuing from the right side). Ch 2, turn. Skip the first st. Work across the 33 sts of the Right Side, chain 28 over the keyhole gap, and work across the 33 sts of the Left Side until 1 st remains. Leave the last st unworked. (94 sts counting the chains)
Rows 14 - 16: Ch 2, turn. Skip the first st. Work across (incorporating the chains on Row 14) following the density map, until 1 st remains. Leave the last st unworked. Each row decreases by 2 stitches (1 at each edge). By Row 16, you will have 88 sts.



4. The Upper Cups (Triangles)
You will split the work again to form the two upper triangles.
Left Upper Cup: - Row 17: With right side facing, attach yarn to the 5th st. Ch 2, work across 40 sts following the density map. (40 sts) - Rows 18 - 30: Ch 2, turn. Decrease 1 st at the armpit edge (outer edge) every row, and alternate decreasing 1 or 2 sts at the cleavage edge (inner edge) to follow the taper of the grid. Continue until Row 30. Fasten off.
Right Upper Cup: - Row 17: Attach yarn to the 52nd st. Ch 2, work across 40 sts following the density map. (40 sts) - Rows 18 - 30: Ch 2, turn. Decrease 1 st at the armpit edge, and alternate decreasing 1 or 2 sts at the cleavage edge. Continue until Row 30. Fasten off.
